track_listing.track: Last Of the New Wave Riders

transcript.text: This very evening, archive sessions from 1970 and 1971 by the original lineup of Mott the Hoofle, and a repeat of one of our recent sets by the band who are going to support Judas Priest on their Spring UK tour, the band called Iron Maiden.
We've got classic cuts by Yes, The Only Ones, ELP, and RDO Speedwagon, and new releases by Utopia, John Fox, Aerosmith, Rush, Girl, Jon and Vangelis, and the Tigers of Pantang.
not forgetting our Friday Night Connection,
the answer to last week's and a new one for this week.
But this week, we start with a new single by Queen.
It started off so well
And that's the new single by Triumph that's just come out,
the Canadian band that is called American Girls.
It's off their album that came out in 1979, entitled Just Again.
And it was especially for Vivian.
And also for a gentleman by the name of Peter Russell
and his future wife, Beverly Sheaf.
It seems they're getting married tomorrow at 12.05 p.m.
And I've got to wish them all the best for the future
and loads of happiness from a gentleman by the name of Philip Russell.
And on the air, I must mention Red Eye.
And it seems that that's some sort of clue to the household,
the number 71 Benson Gardens, Waterloo in Leeds 12.
This came out in 1969, and when I first played it this afternoon,
I hadn't, I think, heard it since then.
And it's delightful. It's yes.
And that's John and Vangelis
And a short track from the album Short Stories
Which is out at the moment on Polydor
The track is called Far Away in Baguade
And before that something from 1969
From the first album ever put out by Yes
It was the track known as Survival
I played it specially for a lady who was all of five years of age when it came out
And I say a lady because she writes a beautiful letter
Her name is Sue
And she lives at number 7 A. Furgrove, Wollaston
Starbridge in the West Midlands
And she writes at the back of her letter here
all about critics and she goes on to knock all the critics because all the critics knock yes
but then she reminds us that tomata was put down by one man writing in sounds but he wasn't right
he got to number three in the melody maker poll honestly sue i think critics half the time don't
know what they're talking about including me when i get on something like round table
it's 10 18 and here's iron maiden good band they were the only ones that came out in 1978 from
their album called the only ones on cbs another girl another planet especially for a gentleman
who writes to us quite a lot.
His name is Chris Sutcliffe
of the number 24 Church Road Witchurch in Cardiff.
Before that, the first of four tonight by Iron Maiden.
It's our session, or one of our sessions tonight.
They actually are going to start a tour,
I believe, on February the 1st.
They'll be touring through March the 2nd.
The first gig they're doing is at Aberdeen University.
On Feb 10th, they're going to be at the Lyceum.
Also on the tour will be Praying Mantis and DJ Neil Kay.
And after that tour, Iron Maiden are going to go on tour
with Judas Priest, hitting the headlines.
Right, let's get down to our archives session now.
Recorded during 1970 and 1971,
here's the original line-up of Mott the Hoople and Three Cuts.
And here's a new progressive crew that makes a welcome addition to the scene,
Mott the Hoople.
This is a number written by groups organist Mick Ralph,
and it's called Thunder Buck Ram.
...Watts, Dale Griffin, Mick Ralphs, Verdon Allen, and Ian Un...
Ah, as we've always called him, Mott the Hoople, recorded in 70 and 71.
three cuts by them
the original Mixed Up Kid, Whiskey Woman and Thunderbuck Ram
more from Mott the Hoople later
you know, we don't spend the rest of the week
sitting around on the wonderful sunny shores of the Serpentine
you know, sort of lazing it
we actually do scurry around the record companies
looking for new product
and this week we've found some
that you're going to like, I know
it's Utopia
phenomenally talented bunch of people
that's Utopia
And a track from an album that's coming out on February the 15th
The album's called Adventures in Utopia
And the cuts that you heard were Last of the New Wave Riders
And Caravan
And that made a gentleman by the name of Matthew Jeffries
Of number 5 Milton Avenue in Bath
Deliriously happy I would think this evening
Because he's been waiting for that album for ages
So let me tell you, it comes out once again on February the 15th
Adventures in Utopia
By Utopia, led of course by Todd Rundgren
13 minutes before 11 o'clock

will not read another filthy limerick, will you, Tony?
